key_insight: LLM-ACES closes the loop between symbolic equation search and new data collection so the model can resolve identifiability gaps and recover the true ODE more reliably.
engineering_relevance: This enables more accurate system identification with fewer measurements, which is valuable for scientific modeling, control, and digital-twin workflows.
method: LLM proposes operator priors to partition the symbolic search space.
Candidate equations are fit within each region and compared for disagreement.
The most informative new trajectories are actively acquired and fed back into the search loop.
evidence_signal: Evaluated on 122 ODE systems from ODEBench and ODEBase, LLM-ACES achieved the lowest median NMSE, high symbolic accuracy (46.2% and 52.4%), and better performance with one-tenth the data.
watch_item: The approach depends on informative active data acquisition and LLM-guided priors, so performance may vary when query access is limited or the prior space is poorly specified.
paper_title: LLM-ACES: Closed-Loop Discovery of Dynamical Systems with LLM-Guided Adaptive Search
